data.title: SBC Medical Group Holdings Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
SBC Medical Group Holdings Inc., a global owner, operator, and provider of management services and products to cosmetic treatment centers, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. The company, headquartered in Irvine, California, and Tokyo, Japan, reported significant financial and operational metrics, reflecting both challenges and growth opportunities.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, SBC Medical reported total revenues of $44 million, a 29% decrease year-over-year. Gross profit for the quarter was $34 million, down 22% from the previous year. Income from operations saw a significant decline of 80%, amounting to $5 million. The company's EBITDA for the quarter was $21 million, representing a 22% decrease, though the EBITDA margin improved to 47% from 43% in the same period last year. Net income attributable to SBC Medical Group was $7 million, a 54% decrease year-over-year, with earnings per share (EPS) at $0.06, down 58% from the previous year.

For the full year 2024, total revenues increased by 6% to $205 million. Gross profit rose by 14% to $156 million. Income from operations slightly decreased by 1% to $70 million. EBITDA for the year was $89 million, an 8% increase, maintaining an EBITDA margin of 43%. Net income attributable to SBC Medical Group was $47 million, an 18% increase year-over-year, with EPS at $0.48, up 14% from the previous year. The company also reported a return on equity of 28%, a decrease of 4 percentage points year-over-year.

Business and Operational Highlights

In 2024, SBC Medical expanded its network to 251 partner clinics, an increase of 43 clinics from the previous year. The number of customers served in the last twelve months reached 6.03 million, a 15% increase year-over-year, with a repeat rate of 71% for customers visiting franchisee clinics twice or more.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

During the year, SBC Medical acquired Aesthetic Healthcare Holdings Pte. and its subsidiaries in Singapore, establishing a strategic footprint in Asia. The company also launched the "SBC Wellness" employee benefit program and entered into a strategic partnership with B4A, a SaaS company for aesthetic clinics in Japan.

Management's Perspective

Yoshiyuki Aikawa, Chairman and CEO of SBC Medical, highlighted the company's solid performance and sustained growth in 2024, despite challenges. He emphasized the company's commitment to expanding its franchising structure and network, both domestically and internationally, to capture significant growth opportunities.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead to FY2025, SBC Medical anticipates continued expansion in the aesthetic dermatology market, though competition is expected to intensify. The company plans to implement strategic price revisions and revise its franchise fee structure to support long-term expansion. The revised fee structure, effective April 2025, aims to alleviate the initial financial burden on franchisees while introducing a tiered fee system aligned with clinic scale and consulting services provided by SBC Medical Group Co., Ltd.

SEC Filing: SBC Medical Group Holdings Inc [ PTWO ] - 8-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: European Medicines Agency Panel Recommends Approval of Five Drugs
data.description:
The European Medicines Agency said Friday its Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use recommended five medicines for approval.

The committee recommended granting a marketing authorization for Averoa's Xoanacyl to treat concomitant hyperphosphataemia and iron deficiency in adults with chronic kidney disease, as well as Santen's Ryjunea to slow the progression of myopia in children.

The agency also adopted positive opinions for biosimilar medicines Jubereq, Qoyvolma and Osvyrti, which are respectively developed by Accord Healthcare, Celltrion Healthcare Hungary and Accord Healthcare. The drugs are for the prevention of skeletal-related events in adults with advanced malignancies; treatment of plaque psoriasis, psoriatic arthritis, Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis; and the treatment of osteoporosis and bone loss.

data.title: Femasys Price Target Maintained With a $15.00/Share by HC Wainwright & Co.
High density polyethylene (HDPE injection) export prices tumble in the United States
data.description: This week, HDPE Injection export prices declined sharply in the US.An industry source in the U.S. informed a Polymerupdate team member, "US spot export prices for high-density polyethylene (HDPE) have decreased across all grades over the past week, driven by a pessimistic market outlook and ongoing uncertainty that has subdued buying interest. Market participants have observed that the current ambiguity regarding possible tariff changes has negatively impacted export sentiment, resulting in a backwardated market structure.

data.title: Top of the Street: Adecco, Michelin, Ferrari
data.description:
A round-up of notable broker activity this morning from Europe's top-ranked* analysts:

** Goldman Sachs upgrades Adecco to "buy" from "neutral" citing an improvement in the staffing firm's early first quarter exit rates. It also highlights Adecco's potential to gain from Germany's fiscal stimulus package and increased defense spending in Europe

** HSBC ups French tyre maker Michelin to "buy" from "hold" saying the company is well-hedged against tariff uncertainties and sees its strong free cash flow supporting higher shareholder returns

** Kepler Cheuvreux raises Italy's Ferrari to "buy" from "hold" viewing the recent sell-off as a favorable opportunity to become more positive on the stock and notes Ferrari's access to a unique clientele that is largely unaffected by current market volatility

(*Analyst rankings from Thomson Reuters StarMine. The scale is from 1-star to 5-star with 5 being the best. Analysts are ranked on earnings accuracy as well as relative performance of recommendations over trailing 12-month & 24-month periods.)

data.title: SNAPSHOT: Chennai market as of March 28, 2025
data.description:
On Friday, the purchase pulse was stable in the open market of Chennai.

PP raffia prices were at Rs.95.00-96.00/kg levels, while PP injection prices were at Rs.95.00-96.00/kg levels. PP film prices were at Rs.97.00-98.00/kg levels. PP Copolymer prices were at Rs.102.00-103.00/kg levels. PP RCP prices were at Rs.107.00-108.00/kg levels.

HDPE raffia prices were at Rs.93.00-94.00/kg levels, while HDPE Injection was at Rs.94.00-95.00/kg levels. HDPE Blow Moulding prices were at Rs.93.00-94.00/kg levels. HDPE Film prices were at Rs.92.00-93.00/kg levels.

LDPE film prices were at Rs.119.00-120.00/kg levels. LLDPE film prices were at the Rs95.00-96.00/kg levels while LLDPE metallocene prices were at the Rs.116.00-117.00/kg levels.

Ethylene-based PVC prices were at Rs.71.00-72.00/kg levels.

(Note: The above prices are basic prices and exclusive of GST)Powered by Polymerupdate's advanced A.I. engine, this news piece represents a seamless blend of generative technology coupled with deep market acumen, thereby setting brand new standards in industry intelligence. Yet another first from the credible Polymerupdate platform.

data.title: CNN THIS MORNING INTERVIEW WITH DAVID LEOPOLD, PARTNER, UB GREENSFELDER AND IMMIGRATION ATTORNEY
data.description:
TRANSCRIPT

March 28, 2025

NEWS PROGRAM

DAVID LEOPOLD, PARTNER, UB GREENSFELDER AND IMMIGRATION ATTORNEY

CNN THIS MORNING INTERVIEW WITH DAVID LEOPOLD, PARTNER, UB

GREENSFELDER AND IMMIGRATION ATTORNEY

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

20 East Thomas Road, Suite 2200

Phoenix, AZ 85012

asc.info@viqsolutions.com

Copyright 2025. Provided under license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

All materials herein are protected by United States copyright law

and/or license from VIQ Media Transcription, Inc., and may not be

reproduced, distributed, transmitted, displayed, published or

broadcast without the prior written permission of

VIQ Media Transcription, Inc.

You may not alter or remove any trademark, copyright or other

notice from copies of the content.

CNN THIS MORNING INTERVIEW WITH DAVID LEOPOLD, PARTNER, UB

GREENSFELDER AND IMMIGRATION ATTORNEY

MARCH 28, 2025

SPEAKERS:

DAVID LEOPOLD, PARTNER, UB GREENSFELDER AND IMMIGRATION

ATTORNEY

AUDIE CORNISH, CNN ANCHOR

AUDIE CORNISH, CNN ANCHOR: I want to turn to President Trump's immigration agenda. That's entering a new phase, arresting legal migrants.

(VIDEO CLIP)

CORNISH: OK, so this is video of Rumeysa Ozturk, Ph.D. student at Tufts in Massachusetts. She was arrested outside her apartment on Tuesday.

What you're looking at are plainclothes immigration officers. They surround her. Some of those officers are wearing masks. It's actually only after she was restrained that they show their badges. She was later driven away in an unmarked car.

The Homeland Security Department alleges that she has supported Hamas, although they did not provide evidence or details. Her arrest, just one piece of a broader effort to deport some foreign nationals studying at American schools.

Data from ICE shows border deportations, they aren't actually keeping pace with Biden administration era. The arrests inside the U.S., of course, are up sharply, 600 percent higher, than at any time during the Biden administration's final year.

We're bringing in immigration attorney David Leopold. He was an advisor to the Biden-Harris transition team on immigration matters.

So, first, let's talk about this idea of going after people who have legal residency. What are you seeing in the arrest of these students?

DAVID LEOPOLD, PARTNER, UB GREENSFELDER AND IMMIGRATION

ATTORNEY: Well, I think the country is learning, Audie, a lesson. And that's that immigration status in the United States, whether the person has a green card, whether the person is visiting, or whether the person is a student, can be very tenuous. There is tremendous authority within the executive branch. The court has deferred to the executive branch. The question really is, what are - what are the enforcement priorities? What is - who are we going after? Why? Are these individuals being - being -

CORNISH: Right. And some of these students and their attorneys are saying, in this case, it's because they spoke out, right, against the war in Gaza.

I want to let you hear Secretary of State Marco Rubio, because he actually says hundreds of student visas have already been revoked. Here's why.

(BEGIN VIDEO CLIP)

MARCO RUBIO, SECRETARY OF STATE: Every time I find one of these lunatics, I take away their visa.

REPORTER: It could - you're saying it could be more than 300 visas?

RUBIO: Sure. I hope. I mean - it will - I mean, at some point I hope we run out because we've gotten rid of all of them.

Why would any country in the world allow people to come and disrupt - we gave you a visa to come and study and get a degree, not to become a social activist that tears up our university campuses. And if we've given you a visa, then you decide to do that, we're going to take it away.

(END VIDEO CLIP)

CORNISH: David, I think citizenship here and everywhere is conditional, right? Some of those conditionals are more - some of those conditions are more stringent than others. What do you make of this argument, though, that do First Amendment sort of protections no longer apply simply because you are not a citizen, right, have that particular designation?

LEOPOLD: Yes. You know, look, the immigration statute itself is extremely complicated. So, let me explain the way this is working. There's section 237 A-4C (ph). Now, it gets very technical, but what that section says is that people - that there are foreign policy grounds to remove people. You cannot remove somebody on mere speech. That's the general rule. The only exception, and this is what we appear to be seeing, is where the attorney - the secretary of state, here Marco Rubio, makes a determination that the individual's presence - it can be mere presence, it can be activities - somehow, potentially, could create adverse foreign policy consequences.

Now, when that law was enacted, the Congress, in their reports, said, this is only to be used sparingly. And it's only to be used - it's not to be used just if somebody disagrees with the government. It's only to be used sparingly.

So, I guess the question for the secretary of state is, is it being used sparingly? Is it - are these unusual circumstances? He, the secretary of state, by law, is supposed to give a reasonable determination. It'd be very interesting to read that and see what —

CORNISH: Yes.

LEOPOLD: — what it said. And that is also supposed to be provided to the members of - to the chairman of the relevant congressional committees.

CORNISH: And there are going to be, obviously, and are underway, legal challenges.

Immigration attorney David Leopold, thank you for this.

END

data.title: G Mining Ventures Swings to Q4 Adjusted Profit
data.description:
G Mining Ventures on Friday reported a swing to a fourth-quarter adjusted profit as the TZ Mine starts production.

The company reported adjusted net income of $58 million, or $0.26 per share, compared with an adjusted loss of $521,000 or $nil per share, in the prior year period.

Quarterly revenue was $102 million, driven by the sale of 39,938 ounces of gold. Revenue reflects commercial production at TZ Mine in Brazil which began last September. G Mining did not book any revenue for the previous corresponding quarter.

"2024 marked a transformational year for GMIN as we executed all phases of our 'Buy. Build. Operate.' Strategy," said Chief Executive Louis-Pierre Gignac. "With just four months of commercial production, TZ is already generating significant free cash flow, giving us the financial flexibility to fund our next phase of growth. The acquisition of Oko West, recognized as one of the top undeveloped gold projects globally, positions us to surpass 500,000 ounces of annual production."

GMIN confirmed its 2025 guidance first released in January.

data.title: Congo doubles salaries for beleaguered army amid rebel advance
data.description:
Democratic Republic of Congo doubled salaries for soldiers and police on Friday, the finance ministry said, in an apparent bid to boost morale as Rwanda-backed M23 rebels advance in the east.

M23 has seized eastern Congo's two largest cities in a swift offensive that has left thousands dead, forced hundreds of thousands to flee their homes and piled pressure on President Felix Tshisekedi.

The pay hikes will be "a turning point towards better conditions" for the army, the finance ministry said.

An officer in North Kivu province told Reuters on Thursday he had heard about the raises, but said troops had not received even their basic salaries over the last two months since banks shut following the fall of the city of Goma.

"We learned that our pay has been doubled, but we haven't yet experienced this increase because our salary has been frozen," the officer said.

Congo has roughly 260,000 soldiers and other security forces, according to the 2025 budget law. Official monthly salaries since 2022 have averaged between $100 and $200 for all ranks, government documents show.

It was not immediately clear what effect the pay raise would have on the budget.

A draft law amending the budget for 2025 was due to be sent to lawmakers earlier this month but that has not emerged.

"These expenses are being incurred outside the budget. This is serious because no one will be able to monitor the actual numbers and salaries of the military personnel to be paid," Valery Madianga, a Congolese expert on public finances, said.

Rwanda denies allegations from Congo and the U.N. that it supports the M23 with arms and troops, saying its forces are acting in self defence against Congo's army and militias hostile to Kigali.
